hook.text: Should a wireless keyboard be under 'Keyboards' or 'Wireless Devices'? In a polyhierarchy, it lives in both.

buildup.text: Traditional hierarchies put each item in one category. Polyhierarchies let items appear in multiple categories.

discovery.text: Amazon uses polyhierarchy extensively. The same product appears in multiple category paths for different search intents.

twist.text: Polyhierarchies are harder to maintain. Adding a product to five categories means five places to update when it changes.

climax.text: Tags and facets implement polyhierarchy naturally. Items have attributes, and users filter by any combination.

punchline.text: Items belong in multiple places. Tags and facets make polyhierarchy practical.

quiz.question: What is the advantage of polyhierarchy in information architecture?
options: Items appear in multiple categories, matching different user search paths
It reduces the total number of categories needed
